text:
BOTTOM LINE — Energy surged on Middle East supply shock; tech and growth sold off on earnings fears and AI capex concerns. Large caps held better than small caps despite broad weakness in mega-cap Tech and Russell 2000's 1.17% drop. Oil's 7.90% jump pulled Energy +2.29% while Nasdaq slumped 1.01%, signaling a sharp rotation from growth into commodities and defensive value plays amid geopolitical instability and inflation concerns from energy costs.

INDICES — VIX at 17.8, below 20-day MA of 20.3, signaling complacency despite intraday volatility. SPY -0.02%, QQQ -1.01%, IWM -1.17%, DIA -0.56% ranked best to worst. Small caps trailed large by 0.76pp. Cross-asset standout: USO crude +7.90%, dragging broader equities lower as investors fear inflation transmission.

VOLUME — SPY vol_ratio 0.6x (declining volume on selling pressure), QQQ vol_ratio elevated at 1.0x+ across tech names. Gaining vol: GLD 0.9x, IJH/IJR 0.9x (small-mid rotation into value). Losing vol: XLB 0.6x, XLU 0.6x, XLF 0.6x. Declining volume on price drop across mega-cap indices = distribution, not capitulation.

SECTORS — XLE Energy +2.29%, XLRE Real Estate +0.97%, XLK Technology +0.80%, XLV Healthcare +0.26%, XLF Financials +0.14%, XLU Utilities +0.13%, XLY Discretionary -0.15%, XLP Staples -0.19%, XLC Comms -0.40%, XLI Industrials -0.61%, XLB Materials -0.86%. Energy 7 +2.20%, Freight 7 -1.21% led bottom movers.

THE 7s — Top 5: Energy 7 +2.20%, Toll Road 7 +1.62%, Semi 7 +1.00%, Old Staples 7 +0.68%, Software 7 +0.50%. Bottom 5: Old Metals 7 -2.93%, Fab 7 -2.82%, Housing 7 -2.19%, Medtech 7 -1.34%, New Staples 7 -1.26%. Energy 7 led by Exxon/Chevron; Fab 7 (TSM, ASML, INTC) hit hardest on tech selloff; SPOT (Spotify) collapsed 12.43% in New Staples, TER (Teradyne) -19.41% in Fab 7 on guidance concerns.

FED & LIQUIDITY — Next FOMC June 17; CME holds at 100%, cuts at 0%, hikes at 0%. Hawk/Neutral/Dove split: 6 hawks, 5 neutral, 4 doves. USD liquidity stress 49/100 (Normal). CP-Tbill spread spiked to 97th percentile at 0.21% (stress signal in short-term credit). ON-RRP minimal at $0.7B, TGA $1.006T. TED spread benign at 0.09%.

POSITIONING — SPY put-call ratio elevated 1.13 (>1.0 = protection demand rising), QQQ PCR 1.07 (hedging demand strong). Skew positive across SPY +5.5%, QQQ +6.7%, IWM +8.7% — put premium elevated, fear present. GEX flip for SPY at 648 vs maxpain 705; price 7,135.95 near critical pivot. QQQ GEX flip 605 near maxpain 640 — dealer hedging could amplify further tech downside. VIX 17.8 below MA signals complacency, but VXN 23.2 shows tech volatility elevated; MOVE 68.7 below MA 74.5 — bond vol has eased despite equity stress.

CROSS-ASSET — Oil +7.90% on Strait of Hormuz closure from Middle East conflict driving Energy sector outperformance; concurrent flight from growth to energy/staples. Gold fell 1.86% despite risk-off — unusual divergence suggests inflation fears (oil) outweighing safe-haven demand. Silver down 2.05% reinforces energy-led inflation narrative over geopolitical flight-to-safety.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 58.9/100 = Neutral. Top 3 movers: SPOT (Spotify) -12.43% [New Staples 7] distribution on earnings; TER (Teradyne) -19.41% [Fab 7] distribution on capex guidance; USO +7.90% [Oil] accumulation on supply shock. 20d momentum: XLK Technology +19.72% best sector, but XLB Materials +1.98% worst. Mag 7 -0.21% revealing tech fatigue despite 20d strength at +16.46%.

WATCH — SPOT (Spotify, -12.43%, New Staples): Vol spiked 6.1x on earnings distribution; watch $420 support. TER (Teradyne, -19.41%, Fab 7): Vol 4.4x on distribution; recent Susquehanna PT raise to $415 now tested. V (Visa, +8.26%, Toll Road 7): Vol 2.6x on accumulation amid payment flow strength; test $700 breakout.
